events.title: Moth Night at the Schuylkill Center for Environmental Education
events.venue: 8480 Hagys Mill Rd, Philadelphia, PA 19128, USA
events.description:
Get up-to-date information at: https://luma.com/20i7ey81

Address:
8480 Hagys Mill Rd
Philadelphia, PA
United States

This July, join us for nocturnal nature appreciation at the
Schuylkill Center for Environmental Education
We'll set up a bright light that attracts moths and other nocturnal insects. We'll see many species, but we never know exactly which species will show up until they come flapping from the forest!
Meet in front of the Visitor Center for check-in.
Things to bring:
-Snacks & Beverages!
-A chair or blanket
-Bug Spray!
-Headlamps/flashlights
-Art supplies/Cameras/etc.

Hosted by DGS / Skype a Scientist

events.title: The Rally Room at Urban Village
events.venue: Urban Village Brewing Company, 1001 N 2nd St, Philadelphia, PA 19123, USA
events.description:
Get up-to-date information at: https://luma.com/sku1nsq4

Address:
Urban Village Brewing Company
Philadelphia, PA
United States

The Rally Room is a meetup for purpose-driven business owners, creatives, and founders who want real connection. Not performative networking.
Connection first. Business second.
That means we start by connecting as humans before we talk shop.
What to expect
You’ll jump into mini-groups right away using Go Deep cards to spark real conversation
We’ll do quick intros later, once the room already feels warm
Then open mingle time to keep the conversations going
This Rally is hosted at Urban Village Brewing Company in Northern Liberties. It’s one of our favorite spots for the room: cozy, casual, and…

Hosted by Jacob Kleinman Phillips

events.title: Fela Kuti Listening Hour
events.description:
Get up-to-date information at: https://luma.com/7lhzcbte

Address:
5231 Germantown Ave
Upon arrival, buzz Studio 101 or call 856-254-3723
Philadelphia, PA
United States

Mouth of Rain x Soul Fly Club Presents: Listening Hour; Fela Kuti

As an extension of the Black Lesbian Listening Room series, Fela Kuti Listening Hour, the event honors music as archive, history, and lived experience.
This is an invitation into a living archive—where records move between hands, memories surface through speakers, and listening becomes an act of care, preservation, and collective building.
We will have sodas and mingling available. Come vibe with us for an evening full of community and love!
Premium Sodas sponsored by Something & Nothing

Hosted by soul. fly. club. & Mouth Of Rain

Sisterhood Summerween: July Book Gathering
We are keeping it spooky this summer by reading horror/thriller books by female authors of Color. To lighten the mood we are crafting DIY charmed bookmarks and playing bookish games. Arrive ready to meet other ladies who enjoy reading and engaging in thought-provoking discussion. See you there.
About Sisterhood Between The Pages
We are a book club for Women of Afro-descent across the diaspora. Our mission is to cultivate an intentional space where we bond over the stories of playwrights, authors, and writers. We are dedicated to amplifying Black Joy,…

Hosted by Sisterhood Between The Pages

events.title: Schuylkill Trail Takeover
events.description:
Get up-to-date information at: https://luma.com/xq251zb5

Address:
Phillies Mural
Philadelphia, PA
United States

Hey yall!! Hope you’ve recovered from Pride Month and are ready for our July hike 🤸🏾🤸🏾🤸🏾‍♂️🤸🏾‍♂️ we’re bringing back the scavenger hunt 🔍🔍
This month we’ll be taking over the Scuhylkill Trail on Sunday July 26th from 12-3pm.
We will start with a quick stretch and get started at 12:20!
Make sure to wear comfy clothes, bring water, and a blanket for break/snack time! 💧🥨🧺
📍MEETING POINT: PHILLIES MURAL
There is some free Sunday street parking along Walnut, as well as affordable parking spots across the bridge near World Cafe Live!

Hosted by BGE Philly HUB

Soul Fly Club Presents: Park Craft
Rooted in accessibility, play, and collective care, this gathering transforms a public green space into a temporary open studio where making becomes both personal and communal. The event invites participants of all ages and skill levels—no prior experience required—to engage freely with creative processes in an open, welcoming environment.
Attendees are encouraged to bring anything they are currently working on, whether finished pieces, works in progress, or early ideas that have not yet taken form. For those arriving without a project, a range of materials…

Hosted by soul. fly. club.
